titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 1, no. 52: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 52. (A.R. No. 149 of 1937.) KUMĀRAVAYALŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the south wall of the maṇḍapa infront of the central shrine in the Agnīśvara temple.

It records that Nālāyirattu Muṉṉūṟṟuvaṉ alias Chandraśēkharaṉ Aramaiyindaṇ, the madhyasthan (the arbitrator) of Nandipanma-maṅgalam, a brahmadēyam on the southern bank (by the river Kāvēri) had dedicated his maid servant, whom he had acquired in the 35th year (i.e. 942 A.D. of the ruling king) Ūraṉ Śōlai, her daughter Veḷḷāḷa Pirāṭṭi and the latter's daughter Aramaiyindaṉ Kaṇḍi with the libation of water to the god, Paramēśvarar of the stone temple at Vayalūr for serving as chauribearers as well as for reciting tiruppadiyam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . matiraiyum Īḻamumkoṇṭa kopparakecaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu 4 10 1 Āvatu teṉkarai brahmateyam nan=tipanmamaṅkalattu
madhyattaṉ nālāyirattu muṉṉūṟṟuvaṉ Āṉa can=tiraśekaraṉ Aramaiyintaṉeṉ nāṉ 35 Āvatu kūḻḻāḷ koḷḷa
Eṉ Aṭiyāḷ Ūraṉ colaiyaiyyum Ivaḷ makaḷ veḷḷāḷapirāṭṭiyaiyum Ivaḷ makaḷ Aramaiyiṉtaṉ kaṇṭiyaiyum Uḷḷiṭṭārai U
k kūṟṟattu vayalūrt tirukkaṟṟaḷi parameśvararkku kavarippiṇāvākavum tiruppatiyam pāṭavum ni(r)roṭu Aṭṭik kuṭutteṉ cantiracekaraṉ Aramaiyiṉtaṉeṉ
Digital edition of SII 32, part 1, no. 52 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
